text: The average lifespan of a domestic cat is 12 to 18 years, although some cats have been known to live into their 20s. Indoor cats generally live longer than outdoor cats due to reduced exposure to hazards such as traffic, predators, and disease.

Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants and some other organisms use sunlight to synthesize foods from carbon dioxide and water. Photosynthesis in plants generally involves the green pigment chlorophyll and generates oxygen as a byproduct.

The Great Wall of China was built over many centuries by various dynasties. The most well-known sections were built during the Ming Dynasty (1368-1644). The wall stretches approximately 13,171 miles and was primarily constructed to protect against invasions from northern nomadic groups.
query: when was the great wall of china built

text: Coffee beans are actually the seeds of the Coffea plant, which is native to tropical regions of Africa. The two most commercially important species are Coffea arabica and Coffea canephora (robusta). Brazil is the world's largest coffee producer.
query: how to brew the perfect cup of coffee

text: Type 2 diabetes is a chronic condition that affects the way the body processes blood sugar (glucose). With type 2 diabetes, the body either resists the effects of insulin or does not produce enough insulin to maintain normal glucose levels. Treatment includes lifestyle changes, monitoring, and sometimes medication.

The stock market experienced significant volatility in March 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell more than 2,000 points on March 16, 2020, its largest single-day point drop at the time. Markets eventually recovered as fiscal stimulus measures were implemented.
query: what caused the 2020 stock market crash

text: Mount Everest, located in the Himalayas on the border between Nepal and Tibet, is the tallest mountain on Earth at 29,032 feet (8,849 meters) above sea level. It was first summited by Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay on May 29, 1953.

The Mediterranean diet emphasizes eating fruits, vegetables, whole grains, legumes, nuts, and olive oil. Fish and poultry are preferred over red meat. Research has shown that this diet is associated with reduced risk of heart disease, stroke, and type 2 diabetes.

Python is a high-level, interpreted programming language known for its readability and versatility. Created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991, Python supports multiple programming paradigms including procedural, object-oriented, and functional programming.

The human brain contains approximately 86 billion neurons. Each neuron can form thousands of connections with other neurons, creating an incredibly complex network. The brain uses about 20% of the body's total energy despite accounting for only 2% of body weight.
query: how many neurons are in the human brain
